SEARCH
Package de.jvstvshd.necrify.api.event

Class EventDispatcher

java.lang.Object
de.jvstvshd.necrify.api.event.EventDispatcher
public class EventDispatcher extends Object
The event dispatcher is used to dispatch events to the event bus. Events may be cancelled or dispatched synchronously or asynchronously. Moreover, listeners can be registered and unregistered; or you may directly listen to events.
Since:
1.2.0
See Also:
  • NecrifyEvent
  • EventBus

    Description

    Creates a new event dispatcher that can be customized completely.

  • Constructor Details

    • EventDispatcher

      public EventDispatcher(@NotNull @NotNull org.greenrobot.eventbus.EventBus eventbus, ExecutorService executorService)
      Creates a new event dispatcher that can be customized completely.
      Parameters:
      eventbus - The event bus to use. See the GreenRobot EventBus documentation for more information.
      executorService - The executor service to use for asynchronous event dispatching. Note: this service must not correspond with the event bus' executor service.

    • EventDispatcher

      public EventDispatcher(ExecutorService executorService, org.greenrobot.eventbus.Logger logger)
      Creates a new event dispatcher with the given executor service and logger. This event bus does not log messages if an dispatched event has no subscriber and does not send an event if no subscriber is registered.
      Parameters:
      executorService - The executor service to use for asynchronous event dispatching and for asynchronous event handling execution (only for thread modes ThreadMode.ASYNC and ThreadMode.BACKGROUND).
      logger - The logger to use for logging messages. If null, sysout will be used for logging. It may be better to delegate messages to the platform's logger, for example with the Slf4jLogger.

  • Method Details

    • dispatch

      public void dispatch(NecrifyEvent event)
      Dispatches the given event synchronously. This method will block until all listeners have been executed. Listener methods registered with ThreadMode.ASYNC or ThreadMode.BACKGROUND will be executed asynchronously through the given eventbus executor; thus the method will not block until these listeners have been executed.
      Parameters:
      event - The event to dispatch.

    • dispatchAsync

      public CompletableFuture<Void> dispatchAsync(NecrifyEvent event)
      Dispatches the given event asynchronously. This method will not block until all listeners have been executed. Listener methods registered with ThreadMode.ASYNC or ThreadMode.BACKGROUND will be executed asynchronously; thus the method will not block until these listeners have been executed.
      Parameters:
      event - The event to dispatch.
      Returns:
      A future that will be completed when the event has been dispatched.

    • register

      public void register(@NotNull @NotNull Object listener)
      Registers the given listener to the event bus. The listener will be able to listen to events via subscribed methods. These methods must be annotated with Subscribe and must have a single parameter of the event type.
      Parameters:
      listener - the listener instance to register.

    • unregister

      public void unregister(@NotNull @NotNull Object listener)
      Unregisters the given listener from the event bus. The listener will no longer be able to listen to events.
      Parameters:
      listener - the listener instance to unregister.

    • cancelEvent

      public void cancelEvent(@NotNull @NotNull NecrifyEvent event)
      Cancels the given event. This method will prevent the event from being delivered to any new listeners, though it may already have been delivered to some listeners.

      This method is implicitly called though NecrifyEvent.cancel()

      Parameters:
      event - the event to cancel.
